channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: sports

item.title: Pepperdine golfers overcome wildfires to compete in Augusta
item.description: Jeneath Wong and Eunseo Choi, two golfers from Pepperdine University, have arrived in Augusta, Georgia for the Augusta National Women's Amateur, after narrowly avoiding the recent Malibu wildfires that ravaged their campus in California.
item.dc:creator: Barclay Bishop, Fox 5

item.title: NFL to use camera technology to measure first downs next season, approves more rule changes
item.description: The NFL announced a multitude of rule changes on Tuesday, including a new way to measure first downs for the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Jared Bush, Fox 5

item.title: Coastal Carolina football fans will be able to eat for free at games in 2025
item.description: Coastal Carolina athletics announced Monday it will offer free concession stand items to fans at football home games during the 2025 season.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Moving on, building up: Dan Campbell reflects on Detroit Lions’ season while preparing for future
item.description: Dan Campbell stepped up to the microphone on Tuesday (April 1) to share his thoughts on the Lions’ offseason and what’s to come this season for Detroit.
item.dc:creator: Hobie Artigue, Local 4

item.title: NBA fan leaves game on stretcher after shooting challenge goes awry
item.description: An Atlanta Hawks fan slipped and injured his knee during a fan challenge as the team lost to the Portland Trail Blazers on Monday night.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Jokic scores career-high 61 points, plays nearly 53 minutes in 140-139 double-OT loss to Wolves
item.description: DENVER (AP) — Michael Malone called him Superman. At the moment, Nikola Jokic’s just super-tired. The Denver Nuggets big man can't wait to get some sleep after a career-best 61-point performance in which he played nearly 53 minutes.
item.dc:creator: PAT GRAHAM, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: After cheating death, Dodgers pitcher Dustin May makes his way back to the mound a changed man
item.description: Dustin May allowed just one hit and an unearned run for the Los Angeles Dodgers in his first major league start in nearly two years.
item.dc:creator: Beth Harris, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: GJ Tigers Girls Soccer pours it on Central 10-0
item.description: GRAND JUNCTION, Colo. (KREX) — There was no moment to take a breath at Unity Field Tuesday night. Seemingly every few seconds yielded a breakaway chance for the Grand Junction Tigers' aggressive attack. And many of those chances were cashed in by the Tigers. Ten of them.
item.dc:creator: Big Will Levenson, KREX 5

item.title: Vejmelka makes 33 saves as Utah HC stays in playoff hunt
item.description: SALT LAKE CITY (ABC4 Sports) - Utah is not out of the playoff hunt just yet. Karel Vejmelka made 33 saves in his 18th consecutive start in goal, as the Utah Hockey Club stayed alive in the chase for the NHL playoffs with a hard-fought 3-1 victory over Calgary Tuesday night at the Delta Center.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: US Olympic and Paralympic officials fire coach and director after AP report on sexual abuse
item.description: The U.S. Olympic & Paralympic Committee has fired a coach and a director after The Associated Press reported that the coach was accused of sexually abusing a young biathlete.
item.dc:creator: Martha Bellisle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: King fans 11 in 7-0 win against Guardians as the Padres are off to 1st 6-0 start
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Michael King struck out 11 in five innings and Jackson Merrill homered for the San Diego Padres, who started 6-0 for the first time in their 57-season history by beating the Cleveland Guardians 7-0 on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: BERNIE WILSON, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: Miranda gets big hit as the Twins beat the White Sox 8-3 for their first win this season
item.description: Jose Miranda hit a tiebreaking two-run single in Minnesota's five-run sixth inning, and the Twins beat the Chicago White Sox 8-3 on Tuesday night for their first win of the season.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Jokes abound as Dodgers' Freddie Freeman misses 2nd game while recovering from shower slip and fall
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Freddie Freeman is hearing it after he slipped and fell in the shower, injuring his surgically repaired right ankle and missing his second straight game for the Los Angeles Dodgers. “Freak accident, you can’t really make it up, crazy,” Freeman said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: BETH HARRIS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ovechkin scores No. 891. He needs 3 to tie Gretzky and 4 to pass him
item.description: Alex Ovechkin moved four goals away from breaking Wayne Gretzky’s NHL record after scoring the 891st of his career in the Washington Capitals game against the Bruins in Boston on Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Budenholzer doesn't expect Durant to be available throughout Suns' 3-game trip
item.description: MILWAUKEE (AP) — Kevin Durant isn’t expected to play at all during the Phoenix Suns’ three-game trip after spraining his left ankle in a loss to Houston on Sunday. The Suns began their road swing Tuesday at Milwaukee.
item.dc:creator: STEVE MEGARGEE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At 89, Fred Costello plays the organ on opening day in Rochester just like he has since 1977
item.description: Fred Costello could swing a bat about as well as he could perform a melody as a kid growing up in Syracuse, New York. He wanted to play at ballparks, and figured the best way to reach that dream was through slick fielding and a sweet swing.
item.dc:creator: DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Steelers owner Art Rooney II sees 'positive signs' that Aaron Rodgers will join the team
item.description: PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Steelers owner Art Rooney II says there are “positive signs” that Aaron Rodgers will play in Pittsburgh. The four-time NFL MVP worked out recently with new Steelers wide receiver DK Metcalf after previously visiting the team.
item.dc:creator: ROB MAADDI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Super Bowl champ Richard Sherman joins long list of sports figures whose homes have been burglarized
item.description: MAPLE VALLEY, Calif. (AP) — Former NFL player Richard Sherman is the latest sports figure whose home has been burglarized. The Super Bowl champion posted pictures and a video on social media asking if anyone recognized three armed intruders that broke into his house last weekend.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: No stigma for Bueckers, Van Lith or Fudd. The basketball stars seek out mental health professionals
item.description: TAMPA, Fla. (AP) — Paige Bueckers, Hailey Van Lith and Azzi Fudd all have reached out to a mental health professional for help at one point, seeking assistance to cope with the increasing pressure on college basketball players.
item.dc:creator: DOUG FEINBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Athletics bat boy Stewart Thalblum takes down drone in left field
item.description: WEST SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — When a drone suddenly appeared near the left-field wall at Sutter Health Park on Monday night, veteran Athletics bat boy Stewart Thalblum decided to help thwart it.
item.dc:creator: JANIE McCAULEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Curtis Granderson talks Cam Smith and Kyle Tucker, Will Venable and the Sox identity, torpedo bats
item.description: Cubs' top prospect Cam Smith is set to make his MLB debut this season, while the White Sox are off to a respectable start with a 2-2 record, and the Yankees have introduced torpedo bats, which are technically operating within the guidelines of the current rule book but are causing outrage among fans
item.dc:creator: Chris Boden, WGN 9

item.title: Amalie Arena transforms for NCAA Women's Final Four
item.description: Amalie Arena is all about basketball for the next week, as the NCAA Women's Final Four takes over Tampa with events around the city.
item.dc:creator: Beth Rousseau, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Houston's defense carries Cougars into 7th Final Four with 69-50 March Madness win over Tennessee
item.description: INDIANAPOLIS (AP) — Houston’s relentless, harassing defense had Tennessee's shooters dancing around. Now, the Cougars are two-stepping it back to Texas. L.J.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Kyle Tucker homers as the Cubs hold off the Diamondbacks for a 4-3 win
item.description: Kyle Tucker hit his first home run with the Chicago Cubs, leading his new team to a 4-3 victory over the Arizona Diamondbacks on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Mavericks outlast Bulls 120-119 to pad lead in play-in race
item.description: Klay Thompson scored 20 points, P.J. Washington had 19 and the Dallas Mavericks beat the Chicago Bulls 120-119 on Saturday night.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Moncada and Soriano deliver as the Angels edge the White Sox 1-0
item.description: Yoán Moncada hit an RBI single against his former team in the eighth inning, and the Los Angeles Angels blanked the Chicago White Sox 1-0 on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Walter Clayton Jr. rallies Florida into Final Four with 84-79 March Madness win over Texas Tech
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Coach Todd Golden cut the final strand of the net, waved it to the crowd and performed the famed Gator Chomp. A celebration that seemed completely improbable just a few minutes earlier was in high gear. Florida is heading back to the Final Four. Walter Clayton Jr.
item.dc:creator: JOSH DUBOW, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Utah HC loses to defending champs in OT, 2-1
item.description: SUNRISE, Fla. (AP) - Sam Bennett scored his second goal of the game off an assist from Brad Marchand at 4:18 of overtime to give the Florida Panthers a 2-1 win over the Utah Hockey Club at Amerant Bank Arena on Friday. By earning one point, Utah is now nine points behind St.
item.dc:creator: Dana Greene, ABC 4

item.title: Bulls face the Mavericks on 4-game win streak
item.description: Dallas Mavericks (36-38, 10th in the Western Conference) vs. Chicago Bulls (33-40, ninth in the Eastern Conference) Chicago; Saturday, 8 p.m. EDT BOTTOM LINE: Chicago aims to keep its four-game win streak intact when the Bulls take on Dallas. The Bulls are 14-22 in home games.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Loyola Chicago men's basketball team beats Kent State, advances to NIT semifinals
item.description: CHICAGO (AP) — The Loyola Chicago men's basketball team didn't make the 68-team NCAA Tournament field this season, but the Ramblers are still ramblin' on to a different Final Four.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9

item.title: NCAA Tournament regionals: A look at Chicagoland connections, program history and more for the men's basketball Sweet 16
item.description: The 2025 NCAA Men's Basketball Tournament resumes Thursday night, and it's down to the Sweet 16. It'll be the Elite Eight after the regional semifinals on Thursday and Friday and then the Final Four after the regional finals on Saturday and Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Michael Johnson, WGN 9
